title: Sudan Conflict Update — Apr 20, 2026
summary: RSF General Al Nour Ahmed Adam defected to the Sudanese Armed Forces on April 20 after fleeing RSF detention in North Darfur with approximately 50 loyal fighters — the second major RSF-to-SAF defection since 2024, suggesting growing fractures in RSF command cohesion as the war enters its fourth year. Al Jazeera published a field report from Sudan's only functioning tropical diseases hospital in Omdurman, documenting the collapse of the country's healthcare system: WHO data now records 2,052 people killed in 217+ attacks on health facilities since April 2023, with 37% of Sudan's facilities non-functional. KPI and casualty data updated to reflect the latest WHO healthcare toll figures.

title: Sudan Conflict Update — Apr 19, 2026
summary: Drone strikes on April 16 killed at least 44 civilians across Darfur and Kordofan — 24+ in Umm Dukhun (Central Darfur, 8 munitions fired) and 9 at the El Wahda market in El Fula, West Kordofan (including 6 children) — reflecting the near-daily aerial campaign killing hundreds across RSF-held and contested territories. Map data updated with new strike trajectories and location points for Umm Dukhun and El Fula. As the SAF-RSF conflict enters Day 1100, the military stalemate deepens with neither side achieving decisive advantage: SAF holds northern and central Sudan and the capital, while RSF controls Darfur, most of Kordofan, and is pressing via the Blue Nile front. Year-field corrections applied to April 15, 16, and 17 event records.

title: Sudan Conflict Update — Apr 18, 2026
summary: The US Treasury OFAC sanctioned three individuals and two firms on April 18 for recruiting and deploying Colombian mercenaries — the 'Desert Wolves' drone specialists — to fight for the RSF in Sudan, marking the first direct US action against the RSF's foreign fighter recruitment infrastructure. The sanctions came as the Third Berlin Conference (April 15) concluded with €1.3 billion in international pledges, which the SAF government formally rejected as a sovereignty violation; the humanitarian funding gap remains critical with only 16% of the $2.9 billion UN 2026 appeal disbursed. Economic data updated to reflect the Berlin Conference pledging outcome.

title: Breaking: DW field report on the Nuba Mountains as a fragile front-line refuge — new geogr
summary: DW field report on the Nuba Mountains as a fragile front-line refuge — new geographic and humanitarian angle not covered by existing year-four-anniversary reporting.

title: Breaking: Sudan war enters fourth year with over 150,000 dead and 14 million displaced.
summary: Sudan war enters fourth year with over 150,000 dead and 14 million displaced.

title: Breaking: Sudan enters fourth year of war as officials describe it as an 'abandoned crisis
summary: Sudan enters fourth year of war as officials describe it as an 'abandoned crisis'.

title: Breaking: Charity warns three babies are born into Sudan's war every minute, highlighting
summary: Charity warns three babies are born into Sudan's war every minute, highlighting humanitarian crisis.

title: Breaking: NGOs: Millions in Sudan Surviving on One Meal a Day as Food Crisis Deepens
summary: A coalition of NGOs reported that millions of Sudanese civilians are surviving on just one meal a day as the civil war enters its third year, with violence and siege tactics systematically eroding Sudan's food supply chains.

title: Sudan Conflict Update — Apr 12, 2026
summary: Sudan formally rejected the Third Berlin Conference on April 12 as the Addis Ababa three-day preparatory meeting concluded its final session, with Sudan's ambassador to Germany submitting a formal memorandum to the German Foreign Ministry declaring the April 15 conference a violation of international law and the UN Charter if it proceeds without Sudanese government participation. Simultaneously, Gen. Burhan announced the largest SAF command reshuffle since the war began — reassigning 21 generals across multiple theaters — consolidating Lt. Gen. Yasser al-Atta's appointment as the new Chief of Staff three days before the war's three-year anniversary. Updated UN data confirms 14 million displaced (world's largest displacement crisis) and 33.7 million requiring humanitarian assistance — two-thirds of Sudan's population — as the conflict enters its fourth year with both parties signaling continued military commitment rather than any shift toward ceasefire.

title: Sudan Conflict Update — Apr 11, 2026
summary: A drone strike on a wedding ceremony in Kutum, North Darfur on April 10 killed at least 30 people including 17 children and injured 107; local groups and the RSF attributed the strike to the SAF while the UN Secretary-General condemned the attack as 'unacceptable.' The Third Berlin Conference preparatory meeting in Addis Ababa continued (April 10–12) with Amnesty International issuing an urgent appeal for high-income countries to use the gathering to save lives as the conflict approaches its three-year mark on April 15. UN data published around the same time confirmed Sudan now hosts 14 million displaced — 9 million IDPs and 4.4 million refugees — the world's largest displacement crisis, with the 2026 humanitarian plan only 16% funded against a $2.9 billion requirement. Map data updated to reflect the Kutum strike site and trajectory.

title: Sudan Civil War Tracker Update — Fri Apr 10, 2026
summary: Drone Strike on Kutum Wedding Kills at Least 30 Including 17 Children; UN Secretary-General Condemns Attack.

title: Sudan Conflict Update — Apr 9, 2026
summary: Sudanese political delegates departed for Addis Ababa on April 9 for the April 10–12 preparatory session of the Third Berlin Conference on Sudan, while the National Forces Coordination confirmed its boycott of both the preparatory and main Berlin proceedings, deepening the conference's legitimacy crisis. An African Union assessment delegation led by Special Envoy Belaiche concluded its Khartoum visit recommending the AU resume office operations in the capital — the first AU assessment mission to Khartoum since the AU evacuated at war's outbreak in April 2023. Military sources disclosed 850+ foreign mercenaries embedded with the RSF including Colombian 'Desert Wolves' drone specialists and Russian technical advisors transited through Somalia and Libya, highlighting the conflict's deepening internationalization and the Colombian specialists' contribution to RSF drone warfare against SAF-held towns.

title: Sudan Conflict Update — Apr 8, 2026
summary: SAF drone strike on April 8 killed Hamid Ali Abubakar — commander-in-chief of the RSF-aligned Sudanese Awakening Revolutionary Council (RAC) and a security adviser to Hemedti — at a command meeting in Damrat al-Firdous, Central Darfur, marking one of the highest-value SAF targeted kills in the Darfur theater. SAF simultaneously secured Al-Tukma (7km east of Dilling, South Kordofan) and fully reopened the Dilling–North Kordofan supply road, breaking the RSF-SPLM-N partial siege of the city following weeks of artillery bombardment of residential areas. A concurrent RSF assault on Al-Tumat neighborhood west of Dilling was repelled. International mediators confirmed Berlin Conference preparatory talks for Addis Ababa on April 10–12, ahead of the April 15 ministerial conference, though ongoing boycott threats from multiple Sudanese political factions cloud the process.

title: Sudan Conflict Update — Apr 7, 2026
summary: Multiple Sudanese political factions including the National Forces Alliance and National Forces Coordination escalated their boycott of the Third Berlin Conference on Sudan (scheduled April 15, 2026), citing unfair invitation distribution, exclusion of the SAF-aligned government, and accusations of conference 'hijacking.' Over 40,000 people (~8,100 households) have been newly displaced from the El Fasher area in North Darfur as RSF military operations continue following its October 2025 capture, compounding a crisis the UN characterized as bearing 'hallmarks of genocide.' Lt. Gen. Yasser al-Atta completed his formal assumption as SAF Chief of Staff in an April 6 handover ceremony — the largest SAF command shakeup since the war began — replacing Gen. Othman al-Hussein, with al-Atta known as a vocal critic of UAE and viewed as sympathetic to Islamist factions.

title: Sudan Conflict Update — Apr 6, 2026
summary: SAF repelled a large-scale RSF ground assault on Wadi al-Hout in North Kordofan on April 6, consolidating SAF's territorial gains in the region. MSF issued a formal condemnation on April 4 of the April 3 RSF drone strikes on Al-Jabalain Hospital (10 killed, 7 medical staff) and a concurrent RSF attack on a medical supply depot in Rabak, White Nile State capital. Sudan's SAF military intelligence delegation held security talks with Libya's Government of National Unity in Tripoli on April 5, agreeing to enhance border security cooperation and intelligence sharing to counter UAE-facilitated RSF supply routes through eastern Libya. RSF-SPLM-N forces continued shelling residential areas in Dilling, South Kordofan on April 5, killing 5 civilians and wounding 33 including children, deepening the ongoing humanitarian emergency there.

title: Sudan Civil War Tracker Update — Sun Apr 5, 2026
summary: A Sudanese military intelligence delegation led by Lt. RSF-SPLM-N Artillery Shells Dilling Residential Areas; Civilians Killed and Injured.

title: Sudan Civil War Tracker Update — Sat Apr 4, 2026
summary: MSF Issues Formal Condemnation of Al-Jabalain Hospital Drone Strikes; RSF Also Hit Rabak Medical Depot.

title: Sudan Conflict Update — Apr 3, 2026
summary: On April 3, RSF launched two drone strikes on Al-Jabalain Hospital in SAF-controlled White Nile State, directly hitting the operating theater and maternity ward during a children's immunization campaign and killing 10 people including 7 medical staff, with 19 more wounded. On April 2, SAF repelled a major RSF multi-front ground assault on Dalang in South Kordofan's Nuba Mountains, with tribal tensions within RSF units weakening assault cohesion. Gen. Burhan reshuffled top SAF military command, appointing Gen. Atta as the new chief of staff amid escalating multi-front pressure across Kordofan and Blue Nile State. The hospital strike pushed total documented healthcare attack deaths in Sudan's war above 2,050, adding to a pattern of RSF drone strikes on medical infrastructure in SAF-held territory.

title: Sudan Conflict Update — Apr 2, 2026
summary: Sudan's civil war reached Day 1083 with an SAF targeted drone strike in Nyala on April 1 killing RSF-aligned Ta'asees coalition founder Osama Hassan and narrowly missing RSF parallel 'Prime Minister' Hassan Al Taishy — a rare targeted assassination deep inside RSF-controlled South Darfur. The UN welcomed extension of the critical Adré border crossing through June 30, 2026, noting it has delivered 118,000+ metric tons of aid supporting 3.3 million people, but warned Sudan's 2026 humanitarian plan is only 16% funded against a $2.9 billion requirement. The UN also reported that El Obeid medical services have been suspended for approximately one month, roads to Dilling and Kadugli are too dangerous for aid operations, and at least 4,000 people fled Blue Nile State to Ethiopia in March as RSF-SPLM-N forces continue pressing toward Geissan and Ed Damazin.

title: Sudan Civil War Tracker Update — Wed Apr 1, 2026
summary: SAF Drone Strike Kills RSF-Aligned Ta'asees Founder Osama Hassan in Nyala; Near-Miss on RSF Premier. The United Nations welcomed the Sudanese government's decision to extend authorization for the Adré border crossing with Chad through June 30, 2026. UN Deputy Spokesperson Farhan Haq issued an urgent statement on April 1 calling on both the SAF and RSF to halt attacks on civilians and critical infrastructure.

title: Sudan Conflict Update — Mar 31, 2026
summary: Sudan's civil war reached Day 1081 with the RSF-SPLM-N siege of Dilling (South Kordofan) entering its third day, Habila road severed and basic commodities depleted; the Sudan Doctors Network warned of an El Fasher-scale catastrophe. In Blue Nile, RSF-SPLM-N shifted focus south toward Geissan threatening the Roseires Dam and Ed Damazin, with 73,000+ civilians displaced into the state capital since Eid. MSF published a landmark report documenting 3,396 sexual violence survivors (97% women and girls) treated at MSF Darfur facilities between January 2024 and November 2025, calling RSF use of rape a systematic 'weapon of war.' The UN condemned escalating drone attacks on civilians in Sudan as potentially constituting war crimes, and the MSF sexual violence figure updated the tracker's casualty data.

title: Sudan Civil War Tracker Update — Mon Mar 30, 2026
summary: Dilling Siege Enters Day 3; Habila Road Remains Severed as Commodities Disappear. RSF 'Shifts Ambitions to South' After Blue Nile Gains; Geissan Faces Imminent Assault Threat. The United Nations issued a statement on March 30 condemning 'repeated drone attacks on civilians' in Sudan as constituting a sharp and alarming increase in strikes on civilian-populated areas.

title: Sudan Conflict Update — Mar 29, 2026
summary: Sudan's civil war reached Day 1079 with a major RSF-SPLM-N ground assault on Dilling, South Kordofan, killing at least 14 civilians (including 5 children and 2 women) and wounding 23 over March 28-29; the Sudan Doctors Network warned of an El Fasher-scale catastrophe as the road to Habila was severed. In Blue Nile, 425+ families fled Geissan amid RSF-SPLM-N military buildup threatening Ed Damazin and the Roseires Dam. SAF drone strikes continued on RSF-held Zalingei (IDP camp area hit) and Al Sunut (7 killed at funeral, 39 wounded). The US pushed a UN Security Council humanitarian ceasefire resolution while the UNSC imposed targeted sanctions on four RSF commanders, including Hemedti's brother Abdul Rahim Dagalo, for El Fasher atrocities.

title: Sudan Conflict Update — Mar 28, 2026
summary: Sudan's civil war entered Day 1078 with the RSF-SPLM-N alliance capturing Kurmuk in Blue Nile State on March 23, opening a new front that threatens Ed Damazin, the state capital, and the Roseires Dam. SAF drones struck Lagawa (West Kordofan) twice — March 23 and 27 — killing 30+ in residential areas, while an RSF drone struck Ad Dabbah in Northern State on March 21 killing 6 and damaging power infrastructure. RSF forces massacred 16 civilians in El Fasher on March 25, continuing post-capture atrocities in occupied North Darfur. New UN envoy Pekka Haavisto met SAF chief Burhan in Port Sudan on March 26 without reaching any ceasefire agreement. The total killed in healthcare attacks since April 2023 surpassed 2,000 following the Eid al-Fitr Al Daein hospital strike.

title: Sudan Civil War Tracker Update — Fri Mar 27, 2026
summary: A drone strike attributed to SAF hit a funeral gathering in Al Sunut, West Kordofan on March 27, killing 7 people and injuring 39. SAF drones struck Zalingei (the Central Darfur state capital under RSF occupation) on Friday evening March 27, hitting residential buildings and parts of the Khamsa Daqaiq camp for displaced persons. SAF forces conducted a second aerial strike on Lagawa, West Kordofan, killing at least 15 people according to a medical source.

title: Sudan Conflict Update — Mar 26, 2026
summary: Sudan's civil war escalated dramatically in mid-to-late March 2026 with a devastating surge in drone warfare killing over 500 civilians since January 1, and more than 200 in just the two weeks following March 4. On Eid al-Fitr (March 20), air and drone strikes struck El Daein Teaching Hospital in East Darfur, killing at least 64 people including 13 children — pushing the total killed in healthcare attacks above 2,000 since the war began. On the battlefield, the RSF recaptured the strategic city of Bara in North Kordofan on March 16 and simultaneously seized the border town of Karnoi, continuing its strategy to encircle SAF headquarters at El Obeid. The conflict spilled further into Chad when an RSF drone killed 17 people in the border town of Tine on March 18, prompting President Déby to close Sudan's 1,300 km border and threaten military retaliation.

title: Sudan Civil War Tracker Update — Wed Mar 25, 2026
summary: RSF forces massacred at least 16 civilians in El Fasher, North Darfur, continuing a documented pattern of atrocities since the city's capture in October 2025.

title: Sudan Civil War Tracker Update — Tue Mar 24, 2026
summary: UN News published a report highlighting the Al Daein hospital strike as emblematic of a broader surge in drone attacks on civilians across Sudan.

title: Sudan Civil War Tracker Update — Mon Mar 23, 2026
summary: A joint force of RSF fighters and Sudan People's Liberation Movement-North (SPLM-N, Abdul Aziz al-Hilu faction) captured Kurmuk, a strategic town on the Ethiopian border in Blue Nile State. SAF drones struck residential neighborhoods in Lagawa, a town in West Kordofan under RSF control, killing at least 15 people and wounding 23. Chadian troops opened fire on civilians gathered near the Sudan-Chad border in Um Dukhun, Central Darfur, on Eid al-Fitr, killing 5 people and injuring 14.

title: Sudan Conflict Update — Mar 22, 2026
summary: Sudan's catastrophic civil war enters day 1072 with two formally declared famines in El-Fasher and Kadugli, and 400,000 people facing starvation. OHCHR published findings in February 2026 concluding RSF violations during the capture of El Fasher constitute war crimes. Fighting intensified in Kordofan in early 2026 with almost daily drone strikes on civilian infrastructure including markets and health facilities. The UN Fact-Finding Mission previously concluded the RSF committed genocide against non-Arab Indigenous groups. Sudan hosts the world's largest humanitarian crisis with 24.6 million food insecure and 12.1 million displaced.

title: Sudan Civil War Tracker Update — Sat Mar 21, 2026
summary: WHO Confirms 64 Killed in Al Daein Hospital Strike; International Condemnation Mounts. An RSF drone struck Ad Dabbah, a town in Northern State on the Nile River, killing at least 6 people.

title: Sudan Civil War Tracker Update — Fri Mar 20, 2026
summary: On the first day of Eid al-Fitr, air and drone strikes hit El Daein Teaching Hospital in East Darfur State, killing at least 64 people including seven women and 13 children.

title: Sudan Civil War Tracker Update — Wed Mar 18, 2026
summary: An RSF drone originating from Sudan struck the Chadian border town of Tine, killing 17 people.

title: Sudan Civil War Tracker Update — Mon Mar 16, 2026
summary: RSF Recaptures Bara and Seizes Karnoi; SAF Repels Dilling Assault. OHCHR: 200+ Civilians Killed by Drones Since March 4 in Kordofan and White Nile.

title: Sudan Civil War Tracker Update — Fri Mar 13, 2026
summary: A drone strike killed at least 11 people at Adikong market in West Darfur, near the Chadian town of Adré.

title: Sudan Civil War Tracker Update — Thu Mar 12, 2026
summary: A drone originating from Sudan struck near the Sudan-Chad border region, killing at least 4 people and injuring many more.

title: Sudan Civil War Tracker Update — Wed Mar 11, 2026
summary: RSF Drone Kills 17 at Shukeiri School and Health Clinic, White Nile State.

title: Sudan Civil War Tracker Update — Tue Mar 10, 2026
summary: A drone strike attributed to the Sudanese military struck a lorry carrying civilians in Al-Sunut, West Kordofan, killing at least 50 people including women and children.

title: Sudan Civil War Tracker Update — Mon Mar 9, 2026
summary: An explosive-laden RSF drone struck a university dormitory in Kosti, the capital of White Nile State, injuring seven students, some critically.

title: Sudan Civil War Tracker Update — Sat Mar 7, 2026
summary: Drone strikes attributed to the Sudanese Armed Forces (SAF) on markets in the West Kordofan towns of Abu Zabad and Wad Banda killed at least 40 civilians.

title: Sudan Civil War Tracker Update — Wed Mar 4, 2026
summary: A drone strike attributed to the Sudanese Armed Forces (SAF) hit a market and hospital in the town of Al-Muglad in West Kordofan, killing at least 50 civilians.
